stem: A right circular cone has a volume of 192π cubic feet and a height of 16 feet. What is the radius, in feet, of the base of the cone?
rationale:
Choice C is correct. The volume of a cone is V = (1/3)πr²h. So 192π = (1/3)πr²(16), giving r² = 192(3)/16 = 36, hence r = 6 feet.

Choice A uses an incorrect ratio. Choice B is off by a factor. Choice D doubles the correct radius.
